seo_title: Former Google and Apple Researchers Launch a Startup to Build AI's…
meta_description: A new startup called Trajectory aims to build a platform for AI that can learn continuously, addressing the major barrier to further AI progress.
body_html:
Trajectory aims to build a platform for AI that can learn continuously, addressing the major barrier to further AI progress.

Key Features of Trajectory's Platform

  • Training on real-world user interactions
  • Continuous learning capability
  • Addressing the major barrier to further AI progress

Founders and Investors

Name Role
Ronak Malde CEO and Cofounder
Arjun Karanam Former AI Researcher at Apple
Michael Elabd Former Google DeepMind Robotics Division

Investment and Valuation

$15 million seed round at a $115 million post-money valuation

Led by Conviction, with participation from Bessemer Venture Partners, Radical VC, and BoxGroup.

source.title: Former Google and Apple Researchers Launch a Startup to Build AI’s Missing Feedback Loop
source.summary: A startup called Trajectory has been launched by former researchers from Google and Apple, aiming to create a feedback loop for artificial intelligence (AI) systems. The goal is to enable continuous learning in AI products, which are currently limited by their inability to adapt quickly. The company plans to utilize rapid iteration cycles to achieve this goal.
